metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about symmetry (lines of symmetry, mirror images, and completing figures)

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Understanding equal halves and matching parts
Matching identical shapes and spotting same-different

questions.question: Which of these shapes has 4 equal sides and 4 corners?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides and 4 corners. Knowing shapes well will help you find their lines of symmetry!

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Does not know that a square has all 4 sides equal while a rectangle has only opposite sides equal
commonMistakes.remediation: Review the difference between a square and a rectangle

questions.question: How many sides does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners. The word 'tri' means three!

questions.question: Which shape has no corners and no straight sides?
questions.explanation: A circle has no corners and no straight sides. It is perfectly round! A circle has a special kind of symmetry.

questions.question: Neha folds a square piece of paper exactly in half. What do the two parts look like?
options: One part is bigger than the other
Both parts are exactly the same size and shape
questions.correctAnswerText: Both parts are exactly the same size and shape
questions.explanation: When you fold a square exactly in half, both parts match perfectly. They are equal halves! This is the idea behind symmetry.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinks folding always makes unequal parts
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Does not understand what folding in half means
commonMistakes.remediation: Practice folding paper shapes in half and comparing the two parts

questions.question: True or False: When you cut a circle through the middle, both halves are the same.
questions.explanation: True! When you cut a circle exactly through the middle, both halves are equal. Each half is a semicircle.

questions.question: Ravi drew a line down the middle of a rectangle. He says both sides are equal halves. Is he correct?
options: Yes, both sides are always equal
Only if the line goes through the exact centre
No, rectangles cannot be split into halves
Only if the rectangle is coloured
questions.correctAnswerText: Only if the line goes through the exact centre
questions.explanation: Ravi is correct only if the line goes exactly through the centre of the rectangle. Then both halves match perfectly!

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinks any line creates equal halves
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Does not understand that the line must be placed precisely
commonMistakes.remediation: Draw lines in different positions on a rectangle and check if both parts are equal

questions.question: Which of these pairs shows two shapes that are exactly the same?
options: A big circle and a small circle
Two squares of the same size
A big rectangle and a small rectangle
questions.correctAnswerText: Two squares of the same size
questions.explanation: Two squares of the same size are exactly the same shape and size. They match perfectly! This matching skill helps you check symmetry.

questions.question: Priya cut a heart shape in half. She placed one half on top of the other. What did she notice?
options: They did not match at all
questions.explanation: A heart shape has two halves that match perfectly when placed on top of each other. This means a heart is symmetrical!

questions.question: When you look in a mirror and raise your right hand, which hand appears to go up in the mirror?
questions.explanation: In a mirror, left and right are flipped! When you raise your right hand, it looks like the left hand is going up in the mirror. This is how mirror images work.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinks the mirror shows the same side
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Does not understand that mirrors flip left and right
commonMistakes.remediation: Stand in front of a mirror and try raising different hands to see the flip

questions.question: A butterfly has the same pattern on both its wings. If you draw a line down the middle of the butterfly, what can you say?
questions.explanation: A butterfly has matching patterns on both wings. If you draw a line down the middle, both sides look the same. This is symmetry in nature!

excellent.message: Excellent! You know your shapes and equal halves really well. You are ready to learn about symmetry!

good.message: Good job! You have a solid understanding of shapes and halves. Let's explore the fun world of symmetry!

partial.message: You know some things but need to review shapes and equal halves. Watch the review videos first, then we will start symmetry.

needs_review.message: Let's first review shapes and equal halves. We will watch some fun videos about these topics before starting symmetry.

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We will show you some fun videos about shapes and halves first.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You are all set to learn about symmetry - fun with shapes and mirrors!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try again to check your progress.
